task_completion.description: Verify that the three required audio tracks are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the track headers in the editor window or the mixer strips. There should be three distinct audio tracks named 'Host Mic', 'Soundboard', and 'Remote Guest'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required audio bus is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the mixer strips or track headers for a bus specifically named 'Mix Minus'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct audio files are imported onto the correct tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the timeline/editor view. The 'Host Mic' track should contain an audio region/waveform named 'narration' (or similar), and the 'Remote Guest' track should contain an audio region named 'good_morning'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Host Mic' and 'Soundboard' tracks are routed to the 'Mix Minus' bus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the mixer view (processor box/sends area) or the routing grid. The 'Host Mic' and 'Soundboard' tracks should have an active send or connection routed to the 'Mix Minus' bus, while their main output to the Master bus remains intact.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Remote Guest' track is routed only to the Master bus and not to the 'Mix Minus' bus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the mixer view for the 'Remote Guest' track. It should show its main output going to 'Master' and should NOT have any send or routing connection to the 'Mix Minus' bus.

task_completion.description: Verify that the gain levels for the 'Remote Guest' track and 'Mix Minus' bus are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the faders or numerical gain readouts in the mixer view. The 'Remote Guest' track should display a gain of approximately +6.0 dB, and the 'Mix Minus' bus should display a gain of approximately -3.0 dB.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Ardour session has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Ardour application window title bar. The session name should not have an asterisk (*) next to it, which would indicate unsaved changes.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour application interface open with visible interactions (e.g., mixer view, routing grid, import dialogs), rather than just a terminal or text editor modifying XML files.

integrity.description: Verify that the routing and track creation are genuinely reflected in the DAW interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mixer strips, faders, and routing connections should visually match the required configuration in Ardour's native UI elements.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent running Python scripts or shell commands to directly generate the Ardour session file without opening the application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Required tracks: 'Host Mic', 'Soundboard', 'Remote Guest'. Required bus: 'Mix Minus'. Host audio file: 'narration.wav'. Guest audio file: 'good_morning.wav'. Target 'Remote Guest' track gain: +6.0 dB (tolerance +/- 0.5 dB). Target 'Mix Minus' bus gain: -3.0 dB (tolerance +/- 0.5 dB).
